articles.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Is Already Smashing Box Office Records
articles.summary: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie broke multiple box office records on its opening day, including surpassing the entire Day 1 gross of its predecessor The Super Mario Bros. Movie from 2023. Nintendo's cinematic universe is officially a blockbuster force.
articles.content:
The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is not just doing well. It is doing historically well. On its first day in theaters it already outgrossed what The Super Mario Bros. Movie made on its opening day back in 2023, and that film went on to earn over 1.3 billion dollars worldwide. Let that sink in.

The Super Mario Bros. Movie in 2023 was a massive surprise hit that proved video game movies could actually work at the box office. It was funny, colorful, and beloved by kids and adults alike. Galaxy has the same energy but from the looks of early numbers it has even more momentum behind it.

This is a big deal for gaming culture broadly. When a video game movie franchise can reliably open this big, studios and game developers pay attention. Expect every major gaming IP to have a movie deal in development somewhere right now. Mario has officially opened a door that is not closing anytime soon.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie 2026 film
keyPoints: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie broke box office records including Day 1 earnings
It outgrossed its predecessor The Super Mario Bros. Movie on opening day alone
The 2023 Mario movie earned over 1.3 billion dollars globally setting the bar high
This cements Nintendo's animated film franchise as a major box office force
The success is likely to accelerate video game to movie adaptations across the industry

articles.title: Brady Ebert Arrested for Attempted Murder of Brendan Yates's Father
articles.summary: Brady Ebert, a founding guitarist of hardcore band Turnstile, was arrested after allegedly using his car to run over the father of lead singer Brendan Yates on March 29. This is a shocking act of violence that has rocked one of the most beloved bands in modern hardcore punk.
articles.content:
This one is hard to process. Brady Ebert, a founding member of Turnstile, was arrested Tuesday and charged with attempted murder. According to a statement from the band, Ebert used his vehicle to deliberately run over Brendan Yates's father on March 29, causing severe physical trauma. That is not a falling out between bandmates. That is a violent crime against someone's family.

Turnstile is a Baltimore hardcore band that broke way beyond their scene in recent years. Their 2021 album Glow On got glowing reviews from critics who don't usually touch hardcore, and they toured with huge names and played massive festivals. They were genuinely one of the most exciting bands going. Ebert was a founding member, which makes this even more stunning.

The band has spoken publicly and confirmed what happened. There is no ambiguity here. Ebert is in custody and facing serious charges. Whatever comes next legally, the Turnstile that fans knew is fundamentally changed. Right now the focus should be on Brendan Yates and his family, and hoping his father makes a full recovery.
keyPoints: Brady Ebert, founding guitarist of Turnstile, was arrested for attempted murder
He allegedly used his car to run over lead singer Brendan Yates's father on March 29
Yates's father suffered severe physical trauma
Turnstile issued a public statement confirming the incident
Ebert is a founding member of the band, making this especially shocking for fans

articles.title: Spaceballs 2 Is Officially Coming in April 2027
articles.summary: The long awaited sequel to Mel Brooks's 1987 sci fi comedy Spaceballs now has an official release date of April 2027. Fans of the original have been waiting decades for this and it is finally, actually happening.
articles.content:
Mel Brooks's Spaceballs is one of the most beloved comedy films ever made. Released in 1987, it was a lovingly ridiculous parody of Star Wars and it became a cult classic almost immediately. Brooks has joked about a sequel for years, literally joking about it inside the original film itself. Now in 2026, it is no longer a joke.

The sequel is officially confirmed with a release date locked in for April 2027. Details about the plot and cast are still thin but just getting a confirmed date is huge news for fans who have been waiting nearly 40 years. The original film featured Brooks himself alongside John Candy, Rick Moranis, and Bill Pullman. Who shows up for the sequel will be one of the most watched casting stories of the next 12 months.

Timing matters here too. Parody comedies are having a real moment again and a Spaceballs sequel landing while Star Wars is still a massive cultural force feels perfectly placed. Whether it can capture the magic of the original is the big question. But the hype is already very real.
keyPoints: Spaceballs 2 has an official release date of April 2027
The original 1987 Mel Brooks comedy is a cult classic Star Wars parody
Fans have been waiting nearly 40 years for a follow up
Cast and plot details are still being kept under wraps
The film arrives at a time when comedy sequels and parodies are trending again

articles.title: HarperCollins Is Turning Kids' Books Into AI YouTube Shorts
articles.summary: Publishing giant HarperCollins has signed a multiyear deal to convert children's books into AI generated YouTube Shorts, and the backlash from parents, authors, and child welfare groups is already building. The deal raises real questions about what AI generated content means for the kids who consume it most.
articles.content:
HarperCollins is one of the biggest publishers in the world. It has been in the business of bringing stories to life for over 200 years. Now it has signed a deal to use AI to turn children's books from its catalogue into short videos for YouTube. The format is YouTube Shorts, which are quick vertical videos similar to TikTok clips. The goal is to reach kids where they already spend their time.

But children's advocacy groups are not happy. Their concern is that AI generated content made for young audiences has not been properly studied or regulated. Nobody fully knows how kids respond to AI made videos versus human made ones. Publishers and creators who work in children's media are also raising their hands, worried this sets a precedent that replaces human creativity with automated content at the most impressionable stage of a child's development.

This story sits right at the crossroads of AI, the creator economy, and children's media and that is a very crowded and contested space right now. HarperCollins is betting that AI can extend the life and reach of its books. Critics are betting that is a gamble with the wrong audience. How this plays out could shape how the entire publishing industry approaches AI for years to come.
keyPoints: HarperCollins signed a multiyear deal to make AI generated YouTube Shorts from children's books
The content will appear on YouTube in the short form vertical video format
Children's advocacy groups have raised concerns about AI content aimed at young audiences
Authors and publishing experts are worried about AI replacing human creative work
The deal could set a major precedent for how publishers use AI going forward

articles.title: Two Lost 1965 Doctor Who Episodes Found and Released
articles.summary: Two Doctor Who episodes from 1965 that were believed to be gone forever have been discovered in a private collection and are now available to watch. For fans of the world's longest running sci-fi show, this is like finding buried treasure.
articles.content:
This is a genuinely wild story for anyone who loves TV history. Two episodes of Doctor Who from 1965 have been sitting in a private collection, completely unknown to the public, and they have just been released. These episodes were part of a huge chunk of early BBC programming that was wiped or lost in the 1960s and 70s when the BBC routinely destroyed old tapes to save money and storage space.

Doctor Who is the BBC sci-fi series that started in 1963 and is still going today, making it one of the longest running shows on television anywhere in the world. The early black and white era of the show is especially precious to fans because so much of it no longer exists. Finding even one missing episode is considered a major event in the fan community, so finding two at once is extraordinary.

The BBC has been tracking down lost episodes for decades, and they occasionally turn up in surprising places like overseas TV archives or, as in this case, someone's private collection. Nobody knows yet how these ended up where they did, which honestly makes the story even better. Go watch them this weekend.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Doctor Who missing episodes BBC history
keyPoints: Two Doctor Who episodes from 1965 have been found in a private collection
They were previously thought to be lost forever along with hundreds of other early BBC episodes
The BBC wiped thousands of old recordings in the 1960s and 70s to save space and money
Doctor Who has been running since 1963 making it one of TV's longest running series
The episodes have now been released publicly for fans to watch

articles.title: Lily Allen's West End Girl Gets Even Better
articles.summary: Lily Allen released West End Girl last October, a raw concept album about the collapse of her marriage to actor David Harbour, and now it is getting a major moment. The album is being praised as one of the boldest pop records in years and it deserves every bit of attention it is getting.
articles.content:
Lily Allen dropped West End Girl with just four days of warning last October and it hit like a freight train. The album is a concept record, meaning every song connects to tell one story. In this case the story is the painful breakdown of her second marriage to Stranger Things actor David Harbour. She does not hold back. At all.

What makes West End Girl so striking is the detail. Allen does not just say the marriage failed. She takes you through it room by room, moment by moment. That is a brave thing to do in public. It is also what separates a good breakup album from a truly great one. Think Olivia Rodrigo's Sour or Beyoncé's Lemonade but with a very British edge.

Now the record is getting a fresh wave of praise and it is easy to see why. If you slept on it when it first dropped, this is your sign to go back. West End Girl is the kind of album that gets better every time you listen. Allen has made her most personal and most powerful work yet.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Lily Allen West End Girl album
keyPoints: Lily Allen released West End Girl in October 2025 with almost no warning
The album tells the story of her split from actor David Husband David Harbour
Every song connects as part of one larger narrative
Critics are now calling it one of the best pop albums in recent memory
If you missed it first time around, now is the perfect time to listen

articles.title: Cardi B Teams Up With Zohran Mamdani on Free Childcare Push
articles.summary: Cardi B has joined forces with New York City mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in a viral video pushing for free childcare for NYC families. It is a rare moment where celebrity star power and local politics actually feel like a natural fit.
articles.content:
Cardi B is a Bronx native and has never been shy about speaking up on issues that affect everyday New Yorkers. This week she teamed up with Zohran Mamdani, a New York State Assembly member running to become the next mayor of New York City, to make a video about free childcare. The clip has taken off fast and it is easy to see why. Cardi has a massive platform and Mamdani has been building a reputation as one of the more energetic voices in New York politics right now.

Childcare costs in New York City are some of the highest in the entire country. For a lot of families it is the single biggest expense after rent, and for many people it is the reason they cannot go back to work. Making it free or heavily subsidised is a policy idea that polls well across party lines because the problem is so universal and so painful.

What makes this video work is that Cardi does not feel like she is just doing a favour for a politician. She is from there. She gets it. And Mamdani clearly knows how to connect with people outside the usual political bubble. Whether you are into NYC politics or not, this is a good example of how to actually talk to people about something that matters.
keyPoints: Cardi B starred in a viral video with NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani promoting free childcare
Mamdani is a New York State Assembly member running to be the next mayor of New York City
Childcare costs in NYC are among the highest in the country and are a major issue for families
Cardi B is from the Bronx and has a long history of speaking on issues affecting New Yorkers
The video is gaining serious traction online and bringing new attention to Mamdani's campaign

articles.title: Netflix Price Hikes Ruled Unlawful in Italy
articles.summary: A court in Rome has ruled that Netflix's past price increases were unlawful, potentially allowing millions of Italian users to claim refunds worth hundreds of euros. This is a big deal because it could set a legal precedent that spreads across Europe and puts other streaming platforms on notice.
articles.content:
A Roman court just handed Netflix a pretty significant loss. Judges ruled that the price hikes Netflix rolled out to Italian subscribers were unlawful. That means customers who paid more during those increases could have a real case for getting that money back.

We are talking about refunds potentially worth hundreds of euros per person. Netflix has raised its prices multiple times over the past few years, and Italy is now the first country where a court has said those increases crossed a legal line. If millions of users pile in with claims, the bill for Netflix could get very large very fast.

This story is worth watching closely even if you are not in Italy. Consumer protection laws vary across Europe but courts in one country ruling against a streaming giant can inspire similar action elsewhere. Netflix has not yet said how it plans to respond, but this is exactly the kind of case that could reshape how streaming services handle pricing for a long time.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Netflix price increases Europe consumer rights
keyPoints: A Rome court ruled Netflix's past price increases were unlawful
Italian subscribers could be entitled to refunds worth hundreds of euros
Millions of users may be eligible to make a claim
The ruling could inspire similar legal action in other European countries
Netflix has not yet publicly responded to the decision

articles.title: Linus Tech Tips Really Did Buy a $5 Million Private Jet
articles.summary: Linus Sebastian of Linus Tech Tips revealed that the April Fools joke about buying a luxury Dassault Falcon 900B private jet with gold sinks was actually real. It is a wild flex that instantly became the biggest story in the creator economy this week.
articles.content:
So here is the thing about April Fools jokes from creators. You never really know. Linus Sebastian posted about buying a private jet and everyone laughed it off as a bit. Then he showed the plane. A real Dassault Falcon 900B. Real gold sinks. Five million dollars. Not a joke.

Linus Tech Tips is one of the biggest tech YouTube channels in the world and Linus Media Group has grown into a serious business over the years. Linus Sebastian has built the kind of operation that most creators only dream about. Still, a private jet is a different level entirely and the gold sinks are a very specific choice.

The internet is split on this one. Some people think it is a hilarious power move and completely on brand for someone who reviews luxury tech for a living. Others are raising an eyebrow at the excess. Either way you feel about it, a YouTube creator buying a private jet and announcing it as an April Fools prank is a genuinely surreal cultural moment.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Linus Tech Tips Linus Sebastian YouTube channel
keyPoints: Linus Sebastian confirmed the private jet purchase was real after posing it as an April Fools joke
The plane is a Dassault Falcon 900B and reportedly cost around 5 million dollars
The jet features gold sinks which became a talking point in their own right
Linus Tech Tips is one of the most successful channels in tech YouTube history
The reveal sparked debate about creator wealth and the scale of the modern creator economy

articles.title: San Francisco Protests Demand AI Industry Pump the Brakes
articles.summary: Hundreds of protesters gathered in downtown San Francisco last month to push back against the rapid development of increasingly powerful AI systems. The protest is a sign that public unease about where AI is heading is turning into something louder and harder to ignore.
articles.content:
A large crowd filled the streets of San Francisco recently carrying signs that read Stop the AI Race and Don't Build It. The message was aimed squarely at the tech companies headquartered nearby. People are worried that the AI industry is moving faster than anyone can understand or safely manage. And they are starting to say so out loud.

Silicon Valley has been in an all out sprint to build AI systems that can improve and update themselves with minimal human input. These are sometimes called self building or autonomous AI agents. The idea is that the AI gets smarter on its own. That sounds impressive until you start asking who is in control and what happens if something goes wrong.

The protest is part of a growing global conversation about whether the people building this technology are moving responsibly. Governments, researchers, and now regular citizens are all raising their hands. The frenzy in Silicon Valley is real, and so is the pushback. This story is not going away anytime soon.
keyPoints: Protesters gathered in San Francisco to demand a slowdown in powerful AI development
The focus is on AI systems that can build and improve themselves
Tech companies in Silicon Valley are at the center of public criticism
Concern is growing about who controls these systems and what could go wrong
Public resistance to AI development is becoming louder and more organised

articles.title: Messi and Ronaldo Get the Lego Treatment for World Cup
articles.summary: Lego has launched a World Cup campaign featuring miniaturised brick versions of Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, Vinicius Jr and Kylian Mbappe. It is a fun collision of football royalty and one of the world's most beloved toy brands.
articles.content:
Imagine the four biggest names in football right now but tiny and made of plastic. That is exactly what Lego has done with their new World Cup campaign, turning Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, Vinicius Jr and Kylian Mbappe into miniaturised Lego versions of themselves. It is the kind of campaign that is almost impossible not to smile at.

The timing makes total sense. The 2026 FIFA World Cup is being hosted across the United States, Canada and Mexico, which means football fever is already building in a massive way. Lego tapping into that with four of the sport's most recognisable faces is a smart move. Messi and Ronaldo are two of the greatest players ever, while Vinicius Jr and Mbappe represent the next generation taking over the sport right now.

This is the kind of crossover that wins the internet for a day and probably sells a lot of Lego sets too. If you have even a passing interest in football or just love a well executed brand moment, this campaign is worth a look. Little plastic Ronaldo doing a celebration is genuinely great content.
keyPoints: Lego launched a World Cup campaign featuring Messi, Ronaldo, Vinicius Jr and Mbappe as mini figures
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is being hosted across the USA, Canada and Mexico
The campaign blends football's biggest current stars with one of the world's most popular toy brands
Messi and Ronaldo are widely considered two of the greatest footballers of all time
Vinicius Jr and Mbappe represent the new generation of global football superstars

articles.title: K-Pop's AI Crisis Is Getting Impossible to Ignore
articles.summary: The K-pop industry is facing a growing and genuinely uncomfortable reckoning with artificial intelligence, from AI-generated idols to deepfake content flooding fan spaces. It matters because K-pop is one of the biggest cultural exports in the world right now, and what happens there tends to ripple outward fast.
articles.content:
K-pop has always been a machine. Highly produced, image-obsessed, and built on a very controlled version of celebrity. But AI is pushing that to a place that is starting to feel genuinely unsettling. We are talking about fully synthetic idols, AI-generated voices mimicking real artists, and deepfake videos that fans sometimes cannot tell apart from the real thing.

The industry is in a weird spot because some labels are actually leaning into AI. A few groups have debuted with AI-assisted members or marketed AI-generated content as part of their brand. But at the same time, real artists and their fans are dealing with unauthorized deepfakes and voice clones that spread across social media with almost no accountability. There are no solid rules yet, and that gap is causing real harm.

This is not just a K-pop problem, but K-pop is where it is showing up loudest right now because the fandom culture is so intense and the parasocial bonds between fans and idols run so deep. When you cannot trust whether a video or voice is real, that bond gets corroded. The industry needs to figure this out quickly, because fan trust is literally the product.
keyPoints: K-pop labels are increasingly using AI for music, visuals, and even synthetic idols
Unauthorized deepfakes and AI voice clones of real K-pop artists are spreading online
There are currently no clear industry rules around AI use in K-pop
The deep parasocial culture of K-pop fandom makes the AI trust problem especially acute
What K-pop does with AI could set a precedent for the wider music industry

articles.title: NASA's Artemis II Mission Has a Sailor Moon Connection
articles.summary: Anime fans spotted Sailor Moon imagery inside NASA's control room during coverage of the Artemis II moon mission, and the internet absolutely lost it. It is a small moment but it says something big about how anime has worked its way into every corner of culture, including space exploration.
articles.content:
NASA's Artemis II mission is a historic one. It is sending humans back toward the Moon for the first time in decades, building toward an eventual crewed lunar landing. So the control room footage is naturally getting a lot of attention. What nobody expected was for eagle eyed anime fans to spot a Sailor Moon reference sitting right there among the equipment and monitors.

Sailor Moon is one of the most iconic anime series ever created. The original 1990s show follows a teenage girl named Usagi Tsukino who transforms into a magical warrior and fights evil by moonlight. The moon connection makes the Artemis II crossover feel almost too perfect. NASA has not officially explained the reference but the internet has already decided it is the best thing to happen this week.

This kind of moment is exactly why anime fandom is so powerful right now. The community notices everything and when they find something like this it spreads everywhere fast. Sailor Moon has been around for over 30 years and it is still showing up in the most unexpected places. Even, apparently, in the rooms where humanity plans its return to the Moon.
keyPoints: Sailor Moon imagery was spotted in NASA's Artemis II mission control room
Artemis II is NASA's mission sending humans back toward the Moon
Sailor Moon is a beloved 1990s anime series centred on the Moon
The discovery went viral almost immediately among anime fans
It highlights just how deeply anime culture has embedded itself everywhere

articles.title: McDonald's Turkey Has a Wild New Menu for Gamers
articles.summary: McDonald's in Türkiye has launched a Pro Gamer Menu that comes with a device called Archie, shaped like the famous golden arch, designed to hold your food so you never have to stop playing. It is a fast food and gaming crossover that nobody saw coming but absolutely tracks.
articles.content:
Going AFK means going away from keyboard, basically stepping away from your game. McDonald's in Türkiye has decided that should never have to happen because of something as basic as eating. Their new Pro Gamer Menu comes with a contraption called Archie, shaped like the iconic McDonald's arch, that holds your burger and drink in place so you can eat with one hand and keep gaming with the other.

It sounds absurd but honestly the gaming audience and the fast food audience overlap in a huge way and brands have been trying to crack that connection for years. McDonald's has done gaming collabs before but this one is more committed than most. Archie is a physical product designed specifically around the way gamers eat, and the fact that it is shaped like the arch is a nice touch.

The catch is that it is only available in Türkiye right now, which means most people reading this will never get to try it. But the internet does not care about geographic limits and this thing is already spreading everywhere. Expect this to become a meme template within the week.
keyPoints: McDonald's Türkiye launched a Pro Gamer Menu with a device called Archie to hold your food while gaming
Archie is shaped like the iconic McDonald's golden arch
AFK stands for away from keyboard and means stepping away from your game
The product is currently only available in Türkiye
The campaign is already going viral well beyond its local market
